entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Build Code – November 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Welcome to our November release update! We're excited to present you many powerful new features. Whether you're a seasoned developer or just starting your journey, we have something for you. This update features a range of exciting new capabilities, such as adding explanations to SQL statements for SAP HANA Cloud or an update to our Generative AI capabilities for MDK development.

Join us as we explore the details of what's new!

 

SAP HANA Cloud SQL Statement Explanation

The SAP HANA Database Explorer SQL Console in SAP Build Code offers a new feature for adding explanations to SQL statements for SAP HANA Cloud. The explanations can make it easier to understand complex or unfamiliar SQL queries.

The statement explanation feature is triggered by selecting an SQL statement in the SQL console and choosing “Explain SQL Statement using AI” from the menu.

Screenshot: Choosing “Explain SQL Statement using AI” from the Menu

The explanations are added to a copy of the original statement into the SQL console. The copy that contains the explanations is delimited by “AI generated SQL query explanation begins/ends” block comments.

Screenshot: AI Generated SQL Query Explanation

As with all AI generated content the generated comments should be reviewed and checked for validity. If the comments are useful, the original statement may be discarded or combined with the comments as needed.

 

Generative AI Capabilities for MDK

We are thrilled to announce an exciting update to our Generative AI capabilities for MDK (Mobile Development Kit) development. Previously, these capabilities were only available when a single project was opened at the folder level. We have now enhanced this functionality to support more scenarios. Our latest updates now enable you to use Generative AI features when working with multiple projects listed either in the Projects Explorer or at the workspace level.

For example, if you have multiple projects open in the Projects Explorer and want to generate a page in a specific project using the MDK page generation genie, it will now prompt you to select a service file from various service files in multiple projects and a page will be generated in that specific project. This enhancement provides greater flexibility and convenience when working with multiple projects in MDK development.

Screenshot: MDK page generation with Joule

 

Further readings

  • Announcing a New Guide: Deep Dive into SAPUI5 Apps on SAP BTP

We are excited to share a new comprehensive guide crafted by our colleague Matthias Schmalz, offering profound technical insights into SAPUI5 applications running on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). In this guide, Matthias delves into essential topics such as how to structure complex SAPUI5 applications to avoid common pitfalls, how multiple apps work with a shared reuse library and how to integrate business solutions as content providers into SAP Build Work Zone.

For further details, dive into Matthias' guide Designing UI5 Apps as Business Solution for SAP Build Work Zone and elevate your expertise in SAPUI5 development.

 

  • Explore Our Recent SAP Fiori Tools Release

For a deeper dive into the latest updates and features in SAP Fiori tools, be sure to check out the following blog post: SAP Fiori Tools 2411 Update: Enhanced Full-Stack Development, AI Integration, and More Open Source. This post provides a comprehensive overview of the new features and improvements that combine frontend and backend development, broaden AI-driven functionalities, and further the integration of open-source technologies.

 

  •  How to Upgrade Your Existing Standalone Service Plans

SAP Build Code offers different types of service plans in SAP BTP: trial, free and standard. For the initial setup, a booster helps you set up SAP Build Code for the plan you want to use. But what if you already use an existing standalone service plan and want to upgrade to one of the SAP Build Code service plans?

We created the following group: Update from Current Service Plan to Build-Code Service Plan. This group consists of tutorials that guide you step by step through the upgrade process:

  • Add SAP Build Code standard (Application) plan
  • Create an SAP Build Code Standard (Application) Subscription
  • Add build-code service plan
  • Update Service Plan

Our help documentation provides you a list of different current and target service plans and supported migration scenarios.

 

  • Webinar Series What's New in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08

Interested in discovering the latest innovations in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08? Then join our "What's New in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08" webinar series! This series is divided into seven captivating sessions, each filled with insights from over a dozen experts straight from our SAP HANA & Database Product Management team. Dive deep into key updates and features while gaining practical industry knowledge.

 

For further information on new features, please check our roadmaps: roadmap for SAP Build Code, SAPUI5 Road Map. And have a look at What's New in SAP Build Code, the What's New Viewer for SAPUI5 and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Release Notes. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates for SAP Build Code: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding! 😊

entry.title: CATS on GDPR Compliance
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We have an issue from the Data Protection Team regarding CATS_APPR_LITE and CATS_DA t-codes. As per their statement - if number of hours charged by users are shown to everyone then it is against GDPR. Can you please help us understand if this is really GDPR non-compliance?

Data protection team said the Line Manager can only read and approve the time for their subordinate and the Cost Object Owner can only read and approve the time which booked to their cost object.

This is not happening unless we need to program the logic in CATS. I wonder this kind of requirement why has not been a standard SAP functionality?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner - General Number Settings
entry.content:
Hello @grace_xin 

I know your blog is more than 2 years old, but maybe you still get notified and could have a look into my question?

I've been starting shortly with CVI and one strange thing occurs in my system, where no buffering is set for number range object: BP_PARTNR

I have one specific vendor account group which contains only 1 vendor. This one is supposed to be synchronized into 1 BP with external number assignment. For that, my interval is 600.000 to 600.010.

Initially, I had set it to 600.000 to 600.001, since I expected the one only BP to be created the first number: 600.000 ... however it dumped for an unknown reason.

In the second system copy it worked fine (probably due to the bigger interval), and as well in Dev. and Q system. However: Each and every time, the final number range status is 600.009, even if only 1 single BP got created:

FYI: I checked both above mentioned notes and the setting is definitely "no buffering"! Plus we have only one single application server. The System is ERP 6.0 EHP 8.

So, what is the reason, why the number range status is not 600.001, but it seems it did buffer 10 numbers? There must be another setting somewhere?

Thanks, Martin

entry.title: Work Package creation directly from Incident
entry.content:
Introduction

When using Focused Build in an operative enviroment, customers are getting Incidents occuring on the productive system. Some of them need an Urgent Fix using the Change Request and the Urgent Correction of the Fix Pace. But ist also may happen, that it is an unurgent fix or more a new requirement. So it would be usefull to create a Work Package from that incident and attach it to a requirement instead of using a Fix Pace Request of Change. This article explains how to customize that.

Preconditions

You have a Focused Build running, inclusive the Fix Pace and a configured Incident Management with a customer spefific incident (ZMIN). As follow up document you have the Focused Build Change Request available in your incident. 

 Incident with Follow Up Popup

 

Your goal is to have the Work Package available as well.

Work Package available in Follow up Popup

Customizing

You have to perform the following steps.

1. Adjust copy control

2. Map Texts

3. Follow up document with copy routine

1. Adjust copy control 

Call transaction SPRO and move to the following subtree:

SPRO subtree

Call Copy Control Rules

Insert a new entry for S1IT (Work Package) as shown below.

New Copy Control Entry

2. Map Texts

Now you move into your new entry and adjust the mappings of text fields. Put in the following 2 text field entries. You can also add others if necessary.

 

Map text fields

Please save your entries.

3. Follow up document with copy routine

You now have to make the S1IT (Work Package) available as Follow Up document for ZMIN (Incident).

Move to the following SPRO entry:

SPRO entry for 'Follow Up Documents'

Create the following new entry:

New Follow Up document entry

You now have the Work Package available as Follow Up Document to the Incident.

Follow Up Pop Up with Work Package

Using Work Packages created as Follow Up

When creating such a Work Package you need to fill in the mandatory fields and save the Work Package.

Mandatory fields in Work Package

If you have the 'Open in UI5 App' button available in your current Business Role you can proceed the Work Package there. 

'Open in UI 5 App' button

Otherwise you need to switch your business role in the WEBUI to get the Work Package shown properly. There you have also the UI5 App button.

Switching Business role to Architect

Now you can use the Work Package in UI5.

Work Package in UI5

Assigning Work Package to a Requirement

The created Work Package is a Work Package withoit a requirement. But you can assign to a requirment in the Requirement Management.

Open Requirement Management Tile

Search for your requirement and choose 'Assign Work Package'.

Requriements Management

Put in the Work Package Number and assign the Work Package.

 

Work Package Assignment Pop Up

Your Work Package is now assigned to the Requirement.

Assigned Work Package

 

Conclusion

You are now able to create Work Package out of Incidents and you have a link between these two tickets.

You can close the Incident after the Work Package Creation as the Incident status will not be changed by the Work Package. 

If you have any questions or input, please feel free to comment and share!

You are also encouraged to follow SAP Solution Manager tag here and the Application Lifecycle Management tag here.

entry.title: Re: /SCWM/ISU can't post with external amount
entry.content:
Hi 

We have a note 3445575 - EWM Initial Stock Upload with External Amount in Local Currency. I am writing cause and solution part. Check if you followed it. I also have blog . note sure if it is allowed . please check if it is see and compare your steps

Cause
This functionality is not available in the EWM standard until S/4HANA 2021 release. Before that, the EWM standard design did not foresee any price management. Every stock posting from EWM had to be done based on the existing price data on ERP side.

Resolution
Before upgrading to a higher release, implement BAdI /SCWM/EX_ERP_GOODSMVT_EXT to update the price-related fields AMOUNT_LC and AMOUNT_SV in table CT_GOODSMVT_ITEM for every item to be posted.

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-q-a/initial-stock-upload-with-map-by-disconnecting-ecc-ewm/qaq-p/12693613

https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/how-upload-project-stock-from-ewm-external-amount-local-mishra-byn3f

 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: Active/Active Read enabled option
entry.content:
Hi,

I don't think you can connect to secondary DB via SAPGUI directly.
I did a test in my local system and it works by following below steps.
1. add secondary DB as a secondary connection in DBCO.
2. in DBACOCKPIT, under "System Configuration", add a new system, choose the Database Connection which was created in above step.
3. Then connect to the secondary DB via button "Connect to Database".

Best regards,
Charles

entry.title: Re: In which table RFHA & BELNR exist together for treasury module ?
entry.content: Get below values from VTBFHAPO and CONCAT them. VTBFHAPO-<ALL KEYS FIELDS>{BUKRS+RFHA+RFHAZU+DCRDAT+TCRTIM+RFHAZB} (All values with CONVERSION ROUTINE "ALPHA" i.e. with LEADING ZEROS) Pass it to External Business Transaction ID in TRDV_TRANS_FLOW (TRDV_TRANS_FLOW-EXTBUSTRANSID) Get the Business Transaction ID from TRDV_TRANS_FLOW (TRDV_TRANS_FLOW-BUSTRANSID) Pass Business Transaction ID to TRACV_DOC_ITEM (TRACV_DOC_ITEM-BUSTRANSID) and get TRACV_DOC_ITEM-BELNR

Regards

entry.title: Re: BELNR field is not getting updated in VTBFHAPO table
entry.content:
BELNR is not updated in VTBFHAPO. It's in TRACV_DOC_ITEM. If you want to get BELNR, please follow the below path.

VTBFHAPO --> TRDV_TRANS_FLOW --> TRACV_DOC_ITEM

Get below values from VTBFHAPO and CONCAT them.

VTBFHAPO-<ALL KEYS FIELDS>{BUKRS+RFHA+RFHAZU+DCRDAT+TCRTIM+RFHAZB}

(All values with CONVERSION ROUTINE "ALPHA" i.e. with LEADING ZEROS)

 

Pass it to External Business Transaction ID in TRDV_TRANS_FLOW

(TRDV_TRANS_FLOW-EXTBUSTRANSID)

 

Get the Business Transaction ID from TRDV_TRANS_FLOW (TRDV_TRANS_FLOW-BUSTRANSID)

 

Pass Business Transaction ID to TRACV_DOC_ITEM (TRACV_DOC_ITEM-BUSTRANSID) and get

BELNR(TRACV_DOC_ITEM-BELNR)

entry.title: Re: Cannot Download AdobeForm Template from SAP Business...
entry.content:
Hello,

when you download the template, it downloads the ZIP file. You can extract the ZIP file and look for file with extension .xdp. 
If the ZIP file is empty then there could be different reason.
1) If you are trying to download the form template created by Cloud Application studio, then it may not be possible to do so directly from UI using Business users.
2) if its a standard form, Raise an incident to SAP.

Regards,
Harshal

entry.title: Base64 String Issues When Posting Attachments via ODataV2 API
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on uploading attachments to supplier invoices in SAP using the API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV via SAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration). While I successfully upload the file to SAP, I encounter an issue when attempting to open it—it appears to be corrupted. I suspect that the problem lies within the payload I am sending, specifically in how the file content is being handled.

Here is the JSON body payload I am using:

{
 "AttachmentContent":
  {
    "MimeType": "application/pdf",
    "Content": base64
 }
}

{ "AttachmentContent": { "MimeType": "application/pdf", "Content": base64 } }

How I Generate the base64 File Content in Javascript:

async function fileToBase64(filePath) {
    return await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
       fs.readFile(filePath, (err, data) => {
           if (err) {
               reject(`Error reading file: ${err.message}`);
           } else {
               var base64 = data.toString('base64');
               resolve(base64);
           }
       });
   });
}


The upload to SAP completes without errors.
When attempting to open the file in SAP, it fails, indicating that the file is corrupted or invalid.

Since the OData receiver in CPI only accepts XML payloads, I use a JSON-to-XML converter. The generated XML looks like this:(deletet the base64 string because it is too long anyway)

<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?> 
<AttachmentContentSet> 
<AttachmentContent> 
<MimeType>application/pdf</MimeType> 
<Content>Base64String</Content> 
</AttachmentContent> 
</AttachmentContentSet>

 I would greatly appreciate guidance on this topic.

 Thank you for your help and insights!

entry.title: SAC with BWLive (BW4HANA) - Copy and Paste to prompt/variable does not work for larger set of values
entry.content:
- Copy and Paste in prompt/selection screen/variable in SAC real slow or does not work at all for larger set of values (300 values do not work at all; 100 values take 1:10 min); After Pressing "OK" in Clipboard Pop Up nothing happens, no matter how long we wait.

- Copy and Paste in prompt in AfO real fast for the same query (Same 300 values in less than a second)

- User said Copy and Paste in SAC (more than 300 values) was slow but possible for the same scenario before Q4/2024 update.

- Checked SAP Notes;
- Implemented Note 3542105
- Already implented:
3309773 - User input on the variable screen takes much more time in SAC
3231323 - InA: Improve performance resolving user input on variable prompt
3288382 - INA : Value Help search not working for REST nodes
RSADMIN "Parameter BICS_OPTIMIZED_SAC_VAR_RESOLVE = X" set; Does not make a difference if we change this
- We did a check of 2541557 - "SAP Analytics Cloud with BW live connection - How to keep the BW system in sync with SAC - Which BW support package is recommended?" but there seems to be nothing of relevance

- After Copy and Paste and pressing "OK" CPU usage on notebook does go up considerably. No workload, job, process etc. at all visible on BW System.

I openend a ticket with SAP. Just wanted to check if anybody else experienced similar problems.

Thanks and best regards

entry.title: How to achieve sequencing in SOAP sender messages in SAP CPI.
entry.content:
Hello CPI Experts,
We are receiving messages from SAP S/4 via SOAP Sender adapter.. SOA set up is done in S/4 system.

It is SAP sender Business partner Service used for replicating the supplier and factories.

Issue is if we receive the same message at same time with same millisecond.Duplicate suppliers are created at target system..This is one issue.

Second is target system API doesnt supports multithreading.. So messages should process in Sequence.

Receiver is http call.How to achieve this in CPI?

Regards,

Karthiga

entry.title: Workflow: Deactivation and Deletion of Technical Objects
entry.content:
Workflow: Deactivation of Technical Objects

Technical objects have their own lifecycles and are used in different maintenance processes. With this workflow, you can implement follow-up activities for bills of materials, maintenance plans and items, task lists, maintenance notifications, and maintenance orders when a technical object is deactivated or flagged for deletion. You can determine which individual steps are executed and whether the system performs activity steps automatically or if agents get work items to decide on and then trigger follow-up activities manually.

Together with BRFplus, SAP Business Workflow allows you to implement follow-up activities for the following EAM objects and documents when a technical object is deactivated or flagged for deletion:

  • maintenance plans
  • maintenance items
  • bills of material
  • task lists
  • maintenance orders
  • maintenance notifications

Availabitlity: SAP S/4HANA 1709 FPS01

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e296651f454c4284ade361292c633d69/249008d913084c09bdb5e5c2a7eaeecb.html?locale=en-US&version=1709+001

Configuration:

SAP has provided a standard solution for this which you can activate by navigating to the below configuration.

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide > Plant Maintenance and Customer Service > Master Data in Plant Maintenance and Customer Service > Technical Objects > General Data > Set Workflow for Deactivation/Deletion of Technical Objects

Select the activity “Assign Process Types to Event”.

Activate the required process types for your event. If you want to use EQUI(Equipment) DELETED (when Equipment deleted) event, select the Active checkbox for that event.

In this example, all events were activated when equipment was deleted.

To check the respective setting options, I recommend reading the following blog post:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/workflow-for-deactivation-deletion-of-technical-objects-equipments/ba-p/13412992

Activate the required Triggering Events in the below Workflow Templates(Transaction: PFTC):

Select “Workflow Template” and the Task-Number

Workflow Pattern: 800044

Workflow Pattern: 800058

The standard workflow is activated and can be used!

In addition, you can use BRF+ to define additional functions, such as:

  • Implement checks for individual activities
  • Specify agents responsible for dialog and decision work items.
  • Define inactive maintenance plans as garbage collectors.
  • Determine the status to be set for task lists assigned to deactivated technical objects.
  • Determine the status to be set for bills of material assigned to deactivated technical objects

Example Set Up Multiple Inactive Plans (Garbage Collectors):

Template rulesets support you in creating inactive maintenance plans as garbage collectors. You can determine inactive maintenance plans depending on details of the workflow, the technical object or the maintenance item via decision table.

The following template rulesets are assigned to the following BRF functions and are not enabled:

  • Ruleset RS_GET_INACTIVE_MPLAN is assigned to BRF function EAM_WF_GET_INACT_MPLAN.
  • Ruleset RS_GET_INACTIVE_MPLANS is assigned to BRF function EAM_WF_GET_INACT_MPLANS.
 

 

You copy these template rulesets (and the depending on objects), adjust the copied rulesets to your business needs and finally enable them.

As a first step, create your customer specific BRFplus application

Copy the following template objects to your application.

Path

Object

Targe Object

EAM_WF_SYSTRuleset

RS_GET_INACTIVE_MPLAN

ZRS_GET_INACTIVE_MPLAN

EAM_WF_SYSTRuleset

RS_GET_INACTIVE_MPLANS

ZRS_GET_INACTIVE_MPLANS

EAM_WF_SYSTRule

RL_GET_INACTIVE_MPLAN

ZRL_GET_INACTIVE_MPLAN_DT

EAM_WF_SYSTRule

RL_GET_INACTIVE_MPLANS

ZRL_GET_INACTIVE_MPLANS_DT

EAM_WF_SYSTExpressionDecision Table

DT_GET_INACTIVE_MPLAN

ZDT_GET_INACTIVE_MPLAN

EAM_WF_SYSTExpressionDecision Table

DT_GET_INACTIVE_MPLANS

ZDT_GET_INACTIVE_MPLANS

Open your customer-specific application in the navigation panel (it is displayed automatically if you choose My Applications in the drop-down list of Show), expand it and ensure that all copied objects are displayed in the tree structure.

Choose decision table ZDT_GET_INACTIVE_MPLAN in the tree structure, add a new line to the table (1) and enter your values for the logical system and the inactive maintenance plan (2). You find the corresponding values to be entered in the table column Logical System in View V_TBDLS.

  • Add further lines to the decision table, one for each inactive maintenance plan you want to create
  • Since you want to create garbage collectors for deactivated maintenance items depending on the maintenance strategy, enter the values as in the picture below:
    • Define different inactive maintenance plans for each logical system, one for each maintenance strategy (in this example, the strategies A, B and C) and one additional garbage collector for maintenance plans that have a different maintenance strategy.
    • Enter the values for the maintenance strategy in table column WSTRA.
  • Finally, activate your settings (2).

Choose decision table ZDT_GET_INACTIVE_MPLANS in the tree structure, add new lines to the table (1), enter the values for your logical systems and the inactive maintenance plans (2) and activate your settings (3).

Choose the rule ZRL_GET_INACTIVE_MPLAN in the structure tree, select your decision table ZDT_GET_INACTIVE_MPLAN (1), (2) and activate your rule (3).

Choose the rule ZRL_GET_INACTIVE_MPLANS in the tree structure, select your decision table ZDT_GET_INACTIVE_MPLANS (1), (2) and activate your rule (3).

Choose ruleset ZRS_GET_INACTIVE_MPLAN in the tree structure, open the context menu (1) and choose Select Existing Rule (2).

Select your rule ZRL_GET_INACTIVE_MPLAN (1) and confirm it with Ok (2).

Display the ruleset header (1), enable the ruleset (2) and activate your settings (3).

Choose the ruleset ZRS_GET_INACTIVE_MPLANS in the tree structure, open the context menu (1) and choose Select Existing Rule (2).

Select your rule ZRL_GET_INACTIVE_MPLANS (1) and confirm it with Ok (2).

Display the ruleset header (1), enable the ruleset (2) and activate your settings (3).

 

An extensive How to Guide is available which helps you with concrete examples and how to implement this in your organization. The example here in the blog post is also copied from the How To Guide.

This solution will help you to get more efficient in handling your master data.

entry.title: Re: Hard stop configuration in Charm to prevent change ow...
entry.content:
Hello Saikat,
For that you need to do some customizing in table AIC_SETTINGS2 (transaction SM34).
You need a combination of the action "SAVE_PARTNER" and the consistency check "SAME_USER".
One saves the current user into a defined partner function and the other checks for the 4-eyes principle (segregation of duities).
There's a similar functionality for transaction type ZMHF/SMHF, but there it only issues a warning.



You can use this as an example and do a similar customizing for transdaction type ZMCR.
But instead of only issuing a warning you would use "abort" and set the previous status.

Hope this helps
Richard

entry.title: Re: Migrating from CR9 nowadays

Now the project runs in the VS enviroment, no errors, the CR reports are displayed BUT in cases where CR parameters are used, then records selection according to parameters is not operating, It seems that CR parameters are no passed in the report. entry.title: Summary der Kaffee-Ecke - Wie läßt sich Innovationskultur aufbauen? Tools & Methoden des SAP AppHaus
entry.content:
Karen Detken und Gerd Stumm stellten in der Kaffee-Ecke vom 21. November das Rahmenwerk vom SAP AppHaus sowie konkrete Methoden vor, die auch einen Bezug zu Lernen und Verändungerungsmanagment haben. Die Methoden und Tools sind alle frei nutzbar und in diesem Artikel verlinkt.

Welcome and Warmup

Gerd startete die Session mit einem Warm-up zum Thema Innovation mit der Übung Future Flash, die wir mit ChatGPT gemacht haben. Die Teilnehmenden stellen sich vor, wie ein von der KI vorgestelltes Objekt in 2050 aussehen könnte.

Das Objekt war ein Rucksack und die Ideen gingen von “läuft neben mir her,” über “kann schwimmen”, bis zu “ist 1x1 cm» groß. Dies gab Gerd per Prompt wiederum in ChatGPT ein, wo das Ganze visualisiert wurde.

 

SAP AppHaus

Das SAP AppHaus umfasst ein interdisziplinäres Team mit 30 Personen und 20 Partnern weltweit und hat in den 11 Jahren seit seiner Gründung über 1200 Kundenprojekte durchgeführt. Eines der Ziele ist es, Human Centered Innovation für so viele Menschen und Unternehmen wie möglich zugänglich zu machen.

Fokus ist es, Kunden darin zu unterstützen, eine Innovationskultur aufzubauen. Dabei wird ein Human-Centered Approach to Innovation (HCAI) angewandt– die Menschen und ihre Bedürfnisse stehen bei der Arbeit im Vordergrund mit Unterstützung von Technologie wie der SAP BTP. Es gibt drei App Häuser von SAP (Heidelberg, Berlin und Palo Alto) und weitere von SAP Partnern. Details zum AppHaus findet ihr hier https://apphaus.sap.com/ .

Innovation-Toolkit

Basierend auf den Erfahrungen aus den Projekten und belegt durch viele Studien kristallisierte sich im Laufe der Zeit heraus, dass der wichtigste Faktor für Innovation und Transformation die Unternehmenskultur ist. Das Team vom AppHaus hat daher angefangen, Methoden zu entwickeln, die eine Innovationskultur fördern. Diese Methoden wurden mit verschiedenen Formaten im Innovation-Toolkit zur Verfügung gestellt.

Gerd fragte auch in die Runde, wer schon Methoden des AppHaus genutzt hat: genannt wurde „Scenes“ – weitere Methoden waren noch nicht bekannt.

Gerd stellte anschliessend das Kundenbeispiel von Rolls-Royce Power Systems (MTU) in Kooperation mit dem AppHaus vor: Der Kunde wollte zunächst in einem Projekt zusammenarbeiten, um die Digitalisierung im Unternehmen voranzutreiben und die Usability von IT-Lösungen zu verbessern und war vom Ansatz (Erfahrungen und Methoden) sehr überzeugt. So wurde neben der Beratung auch der gesamte Ansatz weitergegeben, so dass der Kunde sich nachhaltig selbst um seine Innovationskultur kümmern kann.

An der Stelle übernahm Karen und stellte das Toolkit im Detail vor. Dabei  handelt es sich vor allem um eine Library, die die Methoden umfasst sowie auch Links zu Kursen und Artikeln. Die Library ist kostenfrei nutzbar für Kunden, Partner oder Einzelpersonen. Hier findet man komplette Workshop Guides, Templates, Karten, Methodenbeschreibungen, Whiteboard Templates für die hybride und virtuelle Durchführung bis hin zu haptischen Werkzeugen.

Die Methoden sind Teil des Innovation Culture Frameworks, das eine nachhaltige Innovationskultur unterstützt. Um eine langfristige Veränderung herbeizuführen, muss man die Kultur einer Organisation anpassen und verändern. Es reicht beispielsweise nicht, eine neue Technologie, z.B. AI einzuführen oder einen neuen Prozess. Solange die Menschen, die die Kultur massgeblich beeinflussen, nicht auf die Nutzung der neuen Technologie oder auf die neuen Prozesse vorbereitet werden und den Mehrwert der Neuerungen nicht erkennen, wird die Adoption nicht stattfinden.

Das Framework besteht aus folgenden 5 Dimensionen.

People:

Menschen sollten im Mittelpunkt jeder Transformation stehen, denn sie sind es, die diese Transformation möglich machen. Mit dem Menschen sind bestimmte Fähigkeiten, Denkweisen und Kompetenzen gemeint, die zusammenwirken.

Leadership:

Organisationen brauchen eine Führung, die bestimmte Arbeitsweisen unterstützt und genügend Freiraum zum Anwenden neuer Methoden lassen.

Da Organisationen wachsen und kollaborieren, sind diese Freiräume neu zu durchdenken und zu gestalten.

Place:

Innovationsteams brauchen geeignete Orte und Räumlichkeiten, an denen Innovation stattfinden kann, wo man gemeinsam Workshops durchführt, in denen die Möglichkeit der Kooperation geschaffen wird.

Process

Die Process-Säule oder der Process Enabler bezieht sich auf die Notwendigkeit einer strukturierten Art und Weise, um Innovation zu erreichen. In diesem Fall haben wir den Human-Center-Ansatz für Innovation, eine Reihe von Best Practices, die die Innovationsreise und die Schaffung neuer Ideen bis zur Fertigstellung unterstützen.

Technology

Die Technologie ist wichtig, um die Ideen in die Praxis umzusetzen. Die Technologie wirkt erst dann, wenn sie in Verbindung mit den anderen Faktoren eingesetzt wird.

Im Innovations-Toolkit gibt es Methoden, die sich auf jede dieser Säulen konzentrieren.

Karen stellte dann verschiedene Methoden aus dem Toolkit vor wie Spectrum, Voyage, Scenes.

Spectrum aus der People / Leadership Dimension ist eine kollaborative Methode zum Aufbau von Innovationsteams in Organisationen. Die Methode ist so konzipiert, dass sie das Innovationspotenzial eines Teams auf der Grundlage der spezifischen Bedürfnisse des Unternehmens erschließt. In einem ca. 1-tägigen Workshop entwickeln die Teilnehmenden eine Vision sowie eine Mission und prüfen, ob sie über die notwendigen Fähigkeiten und Skills verfügen, um die entwickelte Mission zu erfüllen.

 

Beispiel für Spectrum

Jeder Kreis im Venn-Diagramm stellt z.B. einen Innovationsbereich dar. In diesem Fall stellt der obere Bereich die Attraktivität dar.

Der orange und der grüne Bereich stehen für die Sichtbarkeit.

Der blaue Bereich ist die Machbarkeit.

Bei jeder Innovation muss darauf geachtet werden, dass alle drei Bereiche berücksichtigt werden, damit die Innovation im Laufe der Zeit Bestand hat. Sollte bei der Visualisierung der Kompetenzen im Diagramm deutlich werden, dass bestimmte Kreise sehr leer sind, kann daraus ein Skill-Gap und weitere Maßnahmen abgeleitet werden.

Voyage aus der People / Leadership Säule: Voyage ist ein Workshop-Format, das ebenfalls etwa einen Tag dauert und darauf abzielt, Teams bei der Definition einer Innovationskultur- oder Transformations-Roadmap zu unterstützen, und zum Beispiel zu Beginn von Projekten eingesetzt wird.

Ein Kernkomponente der Methode ist die Nutzung der Change Formel (“Formula for Change” nach David Gleicher, Kathie Dannemiller, Richard Beckhard & Reuben T.Harris)

D x V x  F > R

D = Dissatisfaction, V= Vision, F = First Steps, R= Resistance to Change

Sie beschreibt grundsätzlich die Bedingungen, die notwendig sind, damit in einer Organisation Veränderungen stattfinden und legt fest, dass sich Organisationen und Einzelpersonen verändern, wenn die Unzufriedenheit multipliziert mit der Vision multipliziert mit den ersten Schritten größer und schwerer wiegen als der Widerstand gegen die Veränderung.

Die Formel besagt also, dass die Wahrscheinlichkeit eines erfolgreichen Wandels steigt, wenn die Unzufriedenheit mit der aktuellen Situation, die Klarheit der Vision, die praktischen Schritte und die Überzeugung, den Wandel bewältigen zu können, hoch sind, während die Widerstände gering sind.

Für jede dieser Komponenten gibt es Übungen wie z.B. der Iceberg Modell Canvas (gehört zu D – Dissatisfaction) in der die Teilnehmenden Innovationsbarrieren für die verschiedenen Säulen des Innovation Frameworks diskutieren und priorisieren.

Das Vorgehen besteht aus drei Schichten:

  1. Beobachtbare Verhaltensweisen: Das, was tatsächlich beobachtet wird (z.B. die verspätete Veröffentlichung einer Software).
  2. Strukturen: Verfahren oder Regeln, die das Verhalten beeinflussen (z.B. zusätzliche Funktionen wurden nach der Planung zugesagt).
  3. Denkweise: Überzeugungen und Wünsche, die das Verhalten beeinflussen (z.B. das Team glaubt, dass das Bearbeiten dringender Anfragen sie vertrauenswürdig macht).

Durch die Anwendung dieses Modells können Interventionspunkte identifiziert werden, um Verhaltensweisen zu ändern und zu verbessern.

Karen beschrieb noch die Übungen „Ideal Workplace“ (Teil der Vision) mit den Komponenten Transformation Manifesto Structure, Creating the Roadmap, Identify objectives to reach the vision.

Die Details dazu könnt ihr in den Slides nachlesen.

Die Prozesssäule konzentriert sich auf den Human Centered Approach zur Innovation, der fünf Phasen umfasst: Explore, Discover, Design, Deliver sowie Run & Scale.

Karen und das Team sind hauptsächlich in den ersten drei Phasen aktiv:

  • Explore:  Finden von wertvollen Anwendungsfällen für die Kunden.
  • Discover: Verstehen des Problems durch Interviews mit Endnutzern.
  • Design: Transformation der Anforderungen in Prototypen und PoCs, die getestet werden. Dabei werden Methoden aus den Bereichen Design Thinking und Unternehmensarchitektur (Architekturdenken) verwendet, um die technologische Landschaft des Unternehmens zu verstehen und die Innovationen zu leiten.

Zum Abschluss stellte Karen die Methode Scenes vor, die sie in diesem Kontext in der Prozesssäule nutzen.

Scenes ist ein Storyboarding-Tool, das Teams dabei hilft, eine visuelle Geschichte auf kollaborative Art und Weise zu erstellen, ohne dass man dafür Zeichenkenntnisse haben muss. Scenes kann man sowohl haptisch nutzen als Magnete oder digital mit PowerPoint Templates, um Visualisierungen von User Stories zu entwickeln.

 

Alle Methoden des AppHaus findet ihr hier https://experience.sap.com/designservices/toolkit/methods, Details zu Voyage gibt es brandaktuell hier VoyageWorkshopPackage

Die Toolkits des Apphaus stehen allen kostenfrei zur Verfügung. Karen, Gerd und wir wären für euer Feedback über die Nutzung der Methoden sehr dankbar. Ihr könnt eure Erfahrungsberichte entweder in der SAP Training und Change Community https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-training-and-change-management/gh-p/training-change teilen oder in unserer LinkedIn Gruppe https://www.linkedin.com/groups/8682361/

Im Anschluss diskutierten wir mit den Teilnehmenden offene Fragen. Interessant war, dass Scenes zwar bekannt war, aber nicht mit dem AppHaus in Verbindung gebracht wurde.

Ein Teilnehmer hat Scenes schon vor Jahren für einen Kunden-Workshop mit Erfolg genutzt. Auch gibt es Kunden, die die Scenes Templates für die storybasierte Entwicklung von Lerninhalten z.B. über SAP Enable Now nutzen.

Fragen der Teilnehmenden

Frage: Gibt es Gesamt-Playbook von Euch dazu?

Antwort: Es gibt das online Innovation Framework

 

Frage: fällt da Thema Digital Adoption Platform (DAP) auch in euren Bereich?

Antwort: DAP Plattform sind eher Technologien, die Methoden des AppHaus sind technologieagnostisch

 

Frage: kann man die Phasen, die Karen vorgestellt hat mit den SAP Activate Phasen matchen?

Antwort: Activate ist mehr für die Implementierung im Projekt, die Methoden des AppHaus greifen vorher

 

Weitere Quellen

https://apphaus.sap.com/resource/business-ai-explore-workshop

Nächste Kaffee Ecken findet ihr hier https://events.sap.com/de/sap-change-training/de/home

Längere Beschreibung zum Innovation Toolkit und speziell zum generative AI Exploration Workshop gibt es auch hier als Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/education-newscast/2024/03/04/enc288-the-sap-apphaus-innovation-toolkit-including-the-generative-ai-exploration-workshop-with-karen-detken/

entry.title: Reminders in Extended Notifications are sent individually rather than collectively
entry.content:
I thought I understood how Extended Notifications work, but apparently I do not 😞

I configured a new category by copying WORKFLOW > STANDARD.
The collective message allows grouping only within the same category.
Subscription has granularity N (multiple notifications in one email) and REMINDER_DAYS set to 2.

The delta filter schedule selection is every 3 minutes between 07:00 and 19:00 7 days a week.
The full filter schedule selection is 07:00 Monday, Wednesday, and Friday.

Delivery schedule is every 3 minutes 24 hours a day 7 days a week.

The initial delivery of notifications was as expected, notifications were delivered quickly and with multiple work items in a notification when relevant. 

I expected that the reminders would be delivered according to the full selection schedule, but this is where I obviously have misunderstood how full selection and/or reminders work. I have not received a single full selection message, but I receive two reminder emails every other day. These are "identical" to the original messages. That's spamming, not notifying, so I assume I must have done something wrong in my setup.

The desired outcome is that notifications about new work items should be sent out quickly, but only once, and subsequent reminders should come from the full selection, but only every other day. How must I change my configuration to achieve that?

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Smartchart sorting or filtering isn't triggering correct get request
entry.content:
Smart Chart ( Sorting )

in view : ( with parameter  EntitySet )

<smartchart:SmartChart id="id4SmartChart"

smartFilterId="id4SmartFilterBar" enableAutoBinding="true"

height="67vh" width="100%" header="Auftragsbestand"

persistencyKey="PKeyChart4" showDownloadButton="true"

showDrillBreadcrumbs="true" data:chartQualifier="ChartDefault2"

beforeRebindChart="onSmartChart09BeforeRebindChart"

chartDataChanged="onChartDataChanged"

smartVariant="true" useVariantManagement="true" useChartPersonalisation="true" > 

</smartchart:SmartChart>

 

In Controller :  

onSmartChart09BeforeRebindChart: function (oEvent) {

            const oSmartChart = oEvent.getSource();

            // Set the binding path
            oSmartChart.setChartBindingPath("/Auftrsbestand(P_ExchangeRateType='M',P_DisplayCurrency='EUR')/Results");

            // Use the asynchronous method to access the inner chart
            oSmartChart.getChartAsync().then(function (oChart) {
                if (oChart) {
                    // Bind data to the chart with sorting
                    oChart.bindData({
                        path: "/Auftrsbestand(P_ExchangeRateType='M',P_DisplayCurrency='EUR')/Results",
                        sorter: [
                            new sap.ui.model.Sorter("OpnSlsOrdsForDelivAmtInDspCrcy", true) // true for descending
                        ]
                    });
                } else {
                    console.error("Chart instance not available.");
                }
            }).catch(function (oError) {
                console.error("Error retrieving the chart instance:", oError);
            });
           

        }

entry.title: Re: SAP Tech Bytes: Use Destinations During Local Development - Cloud Foundry Basics #4
entry.content:
Hi @AdilBasha1SAP,

if you run your CAP server and UI5 (or Fiori elements) app in the same process, make sure to use the cds-plugin-ui5. This way, the UI5 Tooling will be hooked into your CAP server, so that the ui5.yaml configuration is being used as well.

The xs-app.json is the configuration file the approuter, it has nothing to do with the CAP server per se - it's a separate application. You can run the approuter locally as well. To have the full service connectivity (that you have on SAP BTP) you will need to add the respective environment variables to the approuter's default-env.json. I did a video about that: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Jbq6zaKEUyY

Hope this helps.

Best, Nico

entry.title: Exciting Updates in SAP Signavio Process Navigator - Q3 and Q4 Enhancements
entry.content:
SAP Signavio Process Navigator has grown even more powerful with a suite of new features rolled out in Q3 and Q4. We’re excited to share with you the improvements that will enhance your experience and help you navigate your business processes with greater ease and efficiency. Let’s dive in!

 

  1. Show Translated Description Based on User Language Setting
  2. Enable Signavio SVG Format Rendering for Solution Process Flow Diagrams
  3. Filter Solution Activities by Applications and Roles
  4. Display Matching Solution Activities Based on Global Search Filters
  5. Enable Hierarchy Structure for Industry Scenarios
  6. Display Usage Statistical Charts on the Home Page
  7. Refinement of Solution Component Filter on Global Search Page
  8. Improve Personalization with Section Order Reset to Default
  9. Filter Solution Processes by 'Set-up Instructions' and "Task tutorial" on the Scenario Detail Page
  10. Improve Search Functionality for Solution Processes by Solution Components
  11. Include Selection and Filter Criteria in Share Link

 

1. Show Translated Description Based on User Language Setting

One of the standout features is the automatic translation of descriptions based on your language settings. This ensures that you can comfortably understand and use SAP processes regardless of your native language.

 

2. Enable Signavio SVG Format Rendering for Solution Process Flow Diagrams

For those who prefer clarity and high-quality visuals, SAP Signavio Process Navigator now supports Signavio SVG format rendering. This means your process flow diagrams will be crisp and detailed, providing a better overall visual experience.

 

3. Filter Solution Activities by Applications and Roles

To streamline your workflow, you can now filter solution activities by applications and roles directly on the process detail page for your solution process flow diagrams. This feature allows for more efficient and targeted process management.

 

4. Display Matching Solution Activities Based on Global Search Filters

Search filters have been enhanced to display matching solution activities on the process detail page. This makes it easier to find the specific activities you need without sifting through unnecessary information.

 

5. Enable Hierarchy Structure for Industry Scenarios

Industry scenarios now support a hierarchical structure, which provides a more organized and logical view of your processes. This structure helps in understanding the relationships and dependencies between different components of a scenario.

 

6. Display Usage Statistical Charts on the Home Page

Gain insight into how your solutions are being used with new statistical charts. These charts, which show data such as 'Visits in Previous 12 Weeks', 'Solution Scenario Access per Month', and 'Solution Process Access per Month', provide valuable insights into your usage patterns and can help in strategic planning.

 

7. Refinement of Solution Component Filter on Global Search Page

The Solution Component filter on the global search page has been refined for better usability. This means you can find the components you need faster and with greater precision.

 

8. Improve Personalization with Section Order Reset to Default

We’ve improved personalization by allowing you to reset the section order to default for the homepage, scenario, and process detail pages. This feature ensures that if you ever make changes you don’t like, you can easily revert to the default setup.

 

9. Filter Solution Processes by 'Set-up Instructions' and "Task tutorial" on the Scenario Detail Page

Filtering solution processes by 'Set-up Instructions' is now possible on the scenario detail page. This helps you quickly access the processes that pertain to specific set-up instructions, making your workflow smoother.

 

10. Improve Search Functionality for Solution Processes by Solution Components

The search functionality for solution processes has been enhanced, particularly by solution components. This means you can find what you need faster and with more accuracy.

 

11. Include Selection and Filter Criteria in Share Link 

We have expanded the share link functionality to include the selection and filter criteria of the scenario detail page and the results of global search. This makes it easier to share specific views and results with your colleagues.

These updates are designed to make your experience with SAP Signavio Process Navigator more intuitive, efficient, and effective. We hope you enjoy these new features and find them beneficial in managing your business processes. Stay tuned for more exciting updates!

Happy navigating!

*The SAP Signavio Process Navigator Team*

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.4
entry.content:
Preliminary versions of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview of the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.4.

You can find this preview on the current Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408.4”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view of What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408.4 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

There is a new commenting feature available for What’s New. Authors can now evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. 

To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: Shipping point change on position in sales order for subsequent deliveries
entry.content: We are facing a problem regarding the shipping point determination in the sales order. We have a shipping point for direct orders (full truck load) which are directly dispatched by the forwarder and are handled differently regarding picking, scheduling etc. The shipping point is set via checking the weight of all positions in the order for the specific shedule line in all positions. Now we are facing a problem if not the whole order is being shipped and later on there is a subsequent delivery. For that delivery we dont have a full truck load and want a different shipping point. So we change again the shipping point in the order (shipping point is on position level and not on schedule line level). This works more or less. But in the end this is a rather complicated construction with a lot of Z-Coding and there are empty holes i have to fix. Before i invest a lot of time, maybe there is a different solution for a scenario like this. Merging the shipping points is no option.

entry.title: ATP not running for sales orders coming in through EDI
entry.content:
Hi,

For orders coming in over EDI it seems that the ATP check is running completely. We get a delivery date on the sales order line, but when we try to create the outbound delivery this is not possible. The error message says the "0 PC of material XXX available", even if we have the setting to be able to create outbounds without stock. If we rerun the ATP check it is then possible to create the outbound, but then the delivery dates will be wrong (as this is most often not discovered the same day as the order is created, but later in the process).

Could there be a setting in the ATP parameters missing or what could be the issue?

Kind Regards,

Cecilia

entry.title: Calculate Benefit Allowance with Comp Info Integration
entry.content:
I have a benefit allowance that is prorated depending on the employee's eligibility date. However, succeeding allowance amounts should be paid in full after.

Example: Transportation allowance = 15,000 every quarter for all employees

Expectation:

  1. Employee becomes eligible in the middle of the month: July 15
  2. Pay comp recurring should show 12,500 for July quarter (Q3 July-Sept) with end date Sept 30
  3. Pay comp recurring should show 15,000 effective in Q4 Oct-Dec

Actual:

  1. Employee becomes eligible in the middle of the month: July 15
  2. Pay comp recurring shows 12,500 for July quarter (Q3 July-Sept)
  3. No end date for 12,500 July entry in pay comp recurring

My rule correctly computes for the prorated amount and displays this amount as of eligibility date in the benefits section and comp info section. However, succeeding full amounts are not shown correctly, hence employee is still going to receive 12,500 in the next quarter instead of the full amount. How can I display #3 correctly in the comp info portlet? I understand the new benefit job only picks up employees with changes in their profile but what if there's no changes?

entry.title: Re: Damaged label in warehouse and need to print one sing...
entry.content:
Hi @castin ,

if you want to print a product label, we recommend printing the Goods Receipt Label  based on the material document. If the label is damaged, it can also be reprinted later from the goods receipt material document.

I am not aware of any other option to print a product label other than via the goods receipt. 

Of course, there is the HU Label which you could design to only print the HU content and not the HU data itself, but this will only work if you are using HUs. A workaround could be, if you are not working with HUs in general, to temporarily pack the product with the damaged label into a handling unit to print the HU label and to then delete the HU again afterwards.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: How to Dynamically Update Workflow Steps in SAP Build Process Automation Based on External API Data?
entry.content:
I am working on a project using SAP Build Process Automation and have encountered a challenge that I hope someone can help with.

The requirement is to dynamically configure workflow steps based on data fetched from an external API. For example, depending on the API response (which might include specific approval levels, user roles, and decision paths), the workflow should adapt in real time to accommodate additional steps or skip certain ones.

Here’s what I have done so far:

  1. Configured an integration step to call the external API and fetch the required data.
  2. Set up dynamic variables within the process context to store API responses.

However, I am facing issues in dynamically:

  • Adding/removing steps from the workflow sequence.
  • Assigning users or roles fetched from the API to these steps.
  • Ensuring that the workflow continues seamlessly after these adjustments.

Does SAP Build Process Automation natively support this kind of dynamic workflow configuration, or would I need to use custom scripting? If scripting is required, can you share examples or best practices to implement this?

Looking forward to your guidance and insights. Any related blogs, documentation, or similar scenarios would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: On what makes a great frontend framework and why UI5 is one

@akuller_q @nicoschoenteich 

here are some details directly from @StefanBeck :

The next LTS SAPUI5 release will be 1.136 (end of May 2025).

Then (SAP?)UI5 2.0 will be released (June 2025)

Workzone enablement Q3/25, so productive use in Q4/25

Major SAPUI5 version 1.x will be supported until 2032

Both major versions will continue to be developed as S/4 Launchpads in particular do not support iframe integration and therefore cannot run multiple versions at the same time.

All dates is, of course, always subject to change.

entry.title: Unable to off Automatic Touch mode in Mobile Rendering from mobile
entry.content:
Unable to off Automatic Touch mode in Mobile Rendering from mobile.

We are currently facing an issue while configuring custom tiles based on the standard tiles of **SAP_TC_SCM_LE_BE_APPS**. Our objective is to execute the LM00 process using Fiori. While the application functions seamlessly on desktop devices—allowing us to scan multiple barcodes with a single field selection—it behaves differently on handheld devices. Specifically, the process requires manual field selection via the touchscreen for each scan, which hinders efficiency.

To address this, we attempted to disable the Automatic Touch mode in Mobile Rendering directly from the mobile device. However, after disabling this mode and navigating back to the previous screen, the Automatic Touch mode re-enables itself automatically.

We would like to inquire if there is a way to permanently disable the Automatic Touch mode, either through developer settings or from the Administrator section. If there is any documentation or guidance available on this matter, we would greatly appreciate your assistance in resolving this issue.

Looking forward to your response.

entry.title: Help required setting up Integration between Dev Goal and LMS (Assign Learning by Competency)
entry.content:
Hi there SAP expert community! Hope you're all well.

I have a question/query for which I hope you can provide me with valuable assistance. I am working as an LMS support on a project involving the implementation of Talent modules and LMS. The question i have is in regards to the standard integration that can exist between CDP (Goals) and Learning.

My understanding is as follows:

1) When you create a personal development goals, you can assign a competency to the said development goals.

2) When the development goal is created, you are then able to click on the three-dots and select 'Assign Learning' within the Goals module.

3) Upon clicking on 'Assign Learning' there appears a window with two tabs on it: 'By Competency' or 'Search Library'.

4) If an Item within the LMS has the same Competency (Attribute) associated with it that is also associated with the Development Goal, then the Item should appear in the window called 'By Competency'.

We have been given access to a Sales Demo where this works (as might be expected). However in the Dev instance I am working in, this integration isn't working. Also i have noticed that no items are appearing in 'Search Library' either.

I had raised a ticket with SAP on this and they mentioned something about Job Profile Builder migrating to Centre of Capabilities (in Provisioning) and then assigning/creating ratings scales before the APM 'Capabilities Synchronisation' is run in the Learning module. Unfortunately I am not familiar wtih this process so I was hoping if someone from the wider community can:

1) Provide me with a concise step-by-step guide on what I need to do to get this standard integration up-and-running

2) Point me to useful resources that expounds on further information on this.

Really appreciate your help and assistance on this. Much appreciated.

entry.title: E-mail address is invalid: Not listed in allowed email domain
entry.content:
You have added abc@xxx.com in the email an fax settings to the allowed sender domain.

Still you getting an error E-mail address is invalid: Not listed in allowed email domain. (KBA: 2618951)

Since a few years we do not send out emails which are not DKIM signed:

see:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/dkim-enablement-for-sender-domains-byd/ba-p/13531014

 

The solution in this case.

Example:

Originally the DKIM was requested for abc@xxx.de

this key is indeed associated to the domain abc@xxx.com

regardless of this you need to add the domain you requested the DKIM for to the allowed sender XXX.DE

entry.title: Re: Cleanup structures in the namespace /1BCDWB/IQ*
entry.content:
I've made some progress (even if I don't have a solution): When a user creates a QuickView, SAP generates a structure and two function modules in the background. The structure is in the namespace /1BCDWB/ and the function modules are in the namespace /1BCDWB/IQ.
If the QuickView is deleted, neither the structure nor the two function modules are deleted.
This can become a problem if the QuickView used a component type that has been deleted in the meantime.
Then an error occurs during a patch in the activation phase.

We have found an SAP note (2717500) in which the report RSAQ_CLEANUP_QUICKVIEWS is mentioned for cleaning. However, this report also only deletes the QuickView or the query but not the structure and not the two function modules.
Maybe someone has an idea how to delete the structure and the function blocks (this is not possible because they are not assigned to a package)

entry.title: Reduce Risk, Increase Adoption: Leveraging SAP SuccessFactors Process Library
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced digital world, HR transformation is not just an IT project but a strategic initiative that can significantly enhance business operations. Implementing a new HR solution like SAP SuccessFactors should aim to simplify processes, increase efficiency, and improve employee engagement. One of the key tools to achieve these goals is the SAP SuccessFactors process library.

This blog post was created based on the content presented at the SAP SuccessFactors Services webinar Reduce Risk, Increase Adoption: Leveraging SAP SuccessFactors Process Library for Effective HR Transformation delivered on Thursday 21st November, 2024 by HR Business Architects Maria Cicciopastore, Marijn van Amelsfort, and Karlo Smojver. 

Why Cloud Solutions are Different

Cloud-based HR solutions like SAP SuccessFactors offer numerous advantages over traditional IT implementations. They provide faster implementation, scalability, and continuous innovation. However, they also come with limitations, such as the inability to customize the software extensively. Instead, organizations must focus on configuring the solution to meet their needs. This shift necessitates a new approach to process design and implementation.

The Role of the SAP SuccessFactors process library

The SAP SuccessFactors Process Library is a comprehensive collection of leading practices and process diagrams developed by SAP experts. It serves as a valuable resource for organizations embarking on HR transformation projects. Here are some key benefits of using the process library:

  1. Jumpstart Transformations: The library provides a starting point for design discussions, helping organizations avoid starting from scratch. By using leading practices, companies can quickly identify fit-gap areas and streamline their processes.

  2. User-Centric Design: The process library emphasizes the importance of keeping the user at the center of process design. This approach ensures that the new HR solution is intuitive and easy to use for all employees, not just HR professionals.

  3. Standardization and Harmonization: By adopting standardized processes, organizations can achieve greater consistency and efficiency across their HR operations. The process library helps in harmonizing processes, making them easier to manage and scale.

How to Get Started

To effectively leverage the SAP SuccessFactors process library,  SAP Services support the customers with the following structured approach:

  1. Preparation: Set up the SAP Signavio environment and upload the latest process library release. Invite stakeholders to review and comment on the diagrams to ensure everyone is aligned before the workshops.
  2. Strategy: Understand what are the key objectives of the transformation, the value driver, and the design principle to follow
  3. Process Design Workshops: Conduct workshops with key stakeholders, including HR teams, business leaders, IT professionals, and external partners. Use the process library as a foundation to design to-be processes that meet business requirements, and use the value drivers and design principle as a guardrail. 
  4. Fit-Gap Analysis: Perform a fit-gap analysis to identify areas where the standard processes need to be adjusted to fit the organization's unique needs, in alignment with the customer strategy. This step helps in the early adoption of best practices and reduces implementation risks.
  5. Consolidation and Handover: After the workshops, consolidate the designed processes and prepare for implementation. Use SAP Signavio's reporting capabilities to document business requirements, key decisions, and changes

Real-World Example

A recent example of leveraging the SAP SuccessFactors Process Library involved a customer facing challenges with their HR transformation. They had a poorly implemented SuccessFactors solution and were unsure about their readiness for additional modules. By conducting a Phase Zero readiness project using the process library, they were able to:

  • Conduct a thorough fit-gap analysis.
  • Gain insights into future HR roles and responsibilities.
  • Clarify business requirements and prepare for the next implementation phase.

This approach empowered the customer to proceed confidently with their HR transformation journey.

Conclusion

The SAP SuccessFactors process library is a powerful tool for organizations looking to reduce risks and increase adoption during HR transformation projects. By leveraging leading practices and a user-centric design approach, companies can achieve faster, more efficient implementations and ultimately enhance their HR operations. Whether you're just starting your HR transformation or looking to optimize your existing processes, the process library offers valuable resources to guide you every step of the way.

Useful links

  • SAP SuccessFactors process library in SAP Signavio:  https://community.sap.com/t5/process-diagrams-in-sap-signavio/sap-successfactors-process-library-in-sap-signavio/ta-p/216161
  • Webinar Recording:   https://play.goconsensus.com/s618489ab
  • Slides:  https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/xkRwLp6?rc=10&doi=SAP1146726

Feel free to share this blog post on the SAP SuccessFactors community pages to help other customers understand the benefits and best practices of using the SAP SuccessFactors Process Library for their HR transformation projects. If you have any questions or need further assistance, don't hesitate to reach out!

entry.title: Dump CONVT_NO_NUMBER during edition smartform
entry.content:
Good morning,

Since couple of days, I have difficulties to modify specific smartforms trough transaction code SMARTFORMS.

Indeed, when I go in change mode on a specific smartform, then I select a node type "TEXT" and text type is "Text Element", then I add a variable like &GV_MASK&, I click on the check button and then I got a dump ( see attachment ) with the the following details :

Category               ABAP Programming Error
Runtime Errors         CONVT_NO_NUMBER
Except.                CX_SY_CONVERSION_NO_NUMBER
ABAP Program           SAPLSTXK
Application Component  BC-SRV-SCR
Date and Time          28.11.2024 10:23:15

 Short Text
     "15&GV_MASK&" cannot be interpreted as a number

 What happened?
     Error in the ABAP Application Program

     The current ABAP program "SAPLSTXK" had to be terminated because it has
     come across a statement that unfortunately cannot be executed.

 What can you do?
     Note down which actions and inputs caused the error.

     To process the problem further, contact you SAP system
     administrator.

     Using Transaction ST22 for ABAP Dump Analysis, you can look
     at and manage termination messages, and you can also
     keep them for a long time.

 Error analysis
     An exception has occurred which is explained in more detail below. The
     exception is assigned to class 'CX_SY_CONVERSION_NO_NUMBER' and was not caught
      in procedure
     "RTF_SET_PATTERN" "(FORM)", nor was it propagated by a RAISING clause.
     Since the caller of the procedure could not have anticipated this
     exception, the current program was terminated.
     The reason for the exception is:
     An attempt was made to interpret value "15&GV_MASK&" as a number.
     As this value contravenes the rules for displaying numbers correctly,
     this was not possible.

I do not know why I have this dump.

Can you please support quite urgently as I am unable to modify /correct smartforms which contains TEXT nodes.

Thank you for your support

Best regards,

Christophe

entry.title: Introducing Warehousing Concept in SAP S/4 HANA Supply Chain Execution
entry.content:
Hi All

I am explaining warehousing in Supply Chain Execution in SAP S/4 Hana

Brief Overview to SAP S/4 Hana and Features

With SAP S/4HANA, SAP is providing a product that represents the next generation of business applications. Simple enterprise software that is designed to help you run simple in the digital economy. SAP S/4HANA supports

Customer in dealing with challenges like integrating business data with data outside of our company and supporting our business processes with artificial intelligence (or AI). The SAP S/4HANA family is completely built on the in-memory platform SAP HANA. Using the potential of the SAP HANA platform, SAP S/4HANA is designed for customer digital business. It provides an instant insight into where your business currently stands. It does this by combining a single source of truth with dynamic planning and analysis capabilities and real-time process support.

With the SAP Fiori user experience and a less complex data model, SAP S/4HANA is designed to run simple. In parallel, it reduces the data footprint of your company.

 The SAP S/4HANA system is also already connected to business networks and company internal collaboration networks like SAP Build Work Zone. And it also prepares us for supporting your processes with SAP Business AI. All of these aspects protect your investment into SAP software by facilitating the next generation of business applications.

SAP S/4 Hana comes with following Products

SAP S/4 Hana on premises

SAP S/4 Hana Public Edition

SAP S/4 Hana Private Edition

SAP S/4HANA contains the below elements

SAP S/4HANA Enterprise Management.

SAP S/4HANA Line-of-Business Products.

SAP S/4HANA Line-of-Business (LoB) Products for specific industries.

SAP S/4HANA Compatibility Packs.

Supply Chain Management Capability in S/4 Hana

 

SAP S/4HANA enables companies to integrate and visualize all supply chain processes from supplier to customer. Intelligence can also be embedded in these processes. Digital supply chain and mission-critical business processes are supported across the enterprise with one single source of live information such as

  • Reduce Days of Inventory
  • Reduction in Revenue Loss Due to Fulfilment Issues
  • Reduced Overall Supply Chain Planning Costs
  • Improved Supply Chain Planning, increasing Productivity
  • Reduced TCO Through Landscape Simplification

Digital Supply Chain Execution

The core functionality provided by SAP S/4HANA can be extended with other (sometimes Cloud based) SAP products, to support the complete logistics network of a company.

Additional SAP products that support the complete supply chain

SAP Global Batch Traceability

SAP AI Core

SAP Yard Logistics

SAP User Experience Strategy

The use of mobile devices (smart-phones and tablets) to perform certain work tasks, is no longer reserved to a special group of experts. Ease of use is a very important characteristic for the end user of such a device. The focus of the user interface (UI) of the device is no longer maximum functionality, but rather a comfortable user experience and thus a focus on the consumer of the device. With this in mind, the business software running behind the device must be adapted and transformed so that it supports the user interface (used within the business roles of an enterprise) to a maximum. In practice this means that SAP S/4HANA and SAP Fiori as its user interface go hand-in-hand, and should be developed hand-in-hand.

Different User Types — Different Requirements

SAP Fiori

SAP Fiori is a collection of apps, which is created based on a specific set of guidelines and rules and that can be started using the SAP Fiori launchpad.

Available Warehouse Management Solutions in SAP S/4 Hana

Warehouse Management LE-WM

The classic SAP ERP solution for warehouse management LE-WM is part of SAP S/4HANA through a Compatibility Package (CP). This provides customers with a limited use right which expires on December 31, 2025. The inclusion of LE-WM in SAP S/4HANA helps customers migrating from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA to start using some of the advanced features of SAP S/4HANA without having to also immediately migrate their warehouse management solution at the same time. For existing customers that do not require complex warehouse processing in their SAP S/4HANA system, with release 1909 of SAP S/4HANA a basic warehouse management solution has been introduced in SAP S/4HANA called Stock Room Management.

Stock Room Management

The main reason for adding Stock Room Management to SAP S/4HANA is to give existing customers of LE-WM an opportunity to keep their existing LE-WM warehouses after 2025 that do not benefit immediately from moving to (embedded) SAP EWM. Stock Room Management is relevant for small warehouses with manual operations that manage stocks on storage bin level. this deployment allows customers to upgrade to SAP S/4HANA without performing a full-fledged WM transformation.

LE-WM features that are not supported in Stock Room Management include

  • Task and Resource Management (WM-TRM)
  • Warehouse Control Unit interface (WM-LSR)
  • Value Added Service (WM-VAS)
  • Yard Management (WM-YM)
  • Cross-Docking (WM-CD)
  • Wave Management (WM-TFM-CP)
  • Decentralized WM (WM-DWM)

SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM)

SAP EWM first emerged as a successor to SAP Logistics Execution Warehouse Management (LE-WM), with added advantages and advanced features such as material flow system (MFS) and Labor Management. The earliest versions of SAP EWM were only available as separate standalone applications until 2016 when SAP embedded the EWM functionality in the  SAP S/4 Hana Suite. SAP EWM can be installed as standalone or be connected to an SAP S/4HANA system.

The solution can be deployed as Embedded or Decentral. In both case for On Premise or running SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private Edition.

Embedded EWM in SAP S/4HANA

Embedded SAP EWM is also available. Embedded means that no separate system is required and therefore SAP EWM can be used as a direct replacement for LE-WM. EWM is in such a set-up completely embedded within the SAP S/4HANA system. By embedding EWM on SAP S/4HANA, clients could take advantage of the SAP HANA in-memory database  and execute high-speed transactions for their warehousing operations. With each new version of SAP S/4HANA, more functionality was added to embedded EWM, including an ever-growing list of innovative SAP Fiori apps and a stripped-down version called stock room management.

The license type differentiation for Basic or  Advanced is only applicable for embedded deployment cases.

To Choose Basic or Advanced Please customized under IMG-EWM-Master Data -Acknowledge Use of Advance Function

Basic Extended Warehouse Management

Basic license type, customers are able to perform the main process end to end such as, Inbound, Outbound, Production Integration, Quality management integration, etc. Below you can find all the process included on basic license:


Inventory Management 

Inbound Processing 

Outbound Processing 

Internal Warehouse Movements

Physical Inventory

Reporting (Warehouse Management Monitor, Analytics)

Resource Management

Quality Management

Production Integration

Integration with Transportation Management (ASR)

IDOC Integration to third-party MFS systems (SAP Note 2873423)

Advanced Extended Warehouse Management

 The advanced license might be required in case of customer would use some features enclosed of the main process, for example, the outbound process designed is required to use Wave management or, the production integration is required to use JIT - Just in Time. It means that the Advanced license type is required in case of use some specific SAP EWM features, such as

Material Flow System (MFS)

Wave Management

Transportation units

Yard Management

Labor Management

Cross Docking

Value Added Services (VAS)

Warehouse Billing

Kitting

Dock Appointment Scheduling (DAS)

Slotting

Cartonization planning

Distribution Equipment

JIT

WIP Tracking

Warehouse Zones for advanced interleaving

License Management for SAP EWM Embedded

 The list of the extended objects for SAP EWM embedded is technically found on table /SCWM/EXT_OBJ. SAP Product Solution Team provide 2 reports for customers perform in their system in order to know which objects is being used on their warehouse system with usage detail

  • Report /SCWM/RP_COMPLIANCE_CHECK is available since S/4HANA 2016 (and higher) and provides the information if the customizing or the licensing  is compliance or non-compliance for each warehouse system.
  • Report /SCWM/RP_LICENCE_SELF_AUDIT is available since S/4HANA 2022 (and higher) and provides by warehouse number a view by month of usage of Basic and Advanced features by total number of items per month, number of average items per day and active working days, compliance or non-compliance from the customizing perspective

 Decentralized EWM in SAP S/4HANA

After SAP launched embedded EWM in SAP S/4HANA 1610, there were a lot of customers who realized that they wanted to use their existing systems but also embedded EWM features in another SAP S/4HANA physical server. The reasoning was this would ensure higher performance levels and warehouse business process continuity in case of any downtime. To cater to such a requirement, SAP introduced a new deployment option known as decentralized EWM in SAP S/4HANA 1809, FPS 01. Prior to this version, embedded EWM used to share the same master data objects which were loaded as part of the same SAP S/4HANA instance and could not interact with the master data of other SAP ERP  or SAP S/4HANA instances.  With the new decentralized deployment, clients can take advantage of current and new innovations in embedded EWM but keep on using separate SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA core systems.

Note- The Decentralize deployment always requires the advanced license type

Types of SAP EWM Warehouses

 

Types of warehouses and their main tasks could be

Production Warehouse

  • Raw-materials handling
  • Integration of quality management
  • Production supply
  • Receipts from production
  • Materials staging and consumption

Distribution Center

  • Complex process and high volume
  • High degree of automation
  • Wave management
  • Slotting and rearrangement
  • Replenishment

Service Parts Warehouse 

  • Low to very high complexity
  • Kitting and value-added services
  • High volatility
  • Serial numbering
  • Heterogeneous product ranges

Cross-Dock or Transit Warehouse 

  • Regional hubs, gateways, and container freight stations
  • Air and ocean freight handling
  • Unit-load devices and containers handling
  • Receive from and sent to airport or seaport (drayage)

Conclusion- It will give a brief idea about the products and warehouse Management which will help to utilize knowledge to choose correct option for warehouse in Supply chain Management.

Reference https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/understanding-license-type-basic-vs-advanced-for-sap-extended-warehouse/ba-p/13580547

SAP Help 

Please provide the feed back to improve it
